behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because the failure is at the STT service boundary, not a local parser. System beats Integration because mocked STT responses would hide real diarization/speaker-label failures from the external API. Counterbalance: Agentic beats System because correctness depends on real model output quality (speaker attribution), which can vary and is not validated by deterministic stubs. Rejection gate: Workflow was considered but rejected because the behavior text targets STT output preservation itself, not multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the real STT API and assert speaker labels are preserved and correctly associated with utterances used for attendee attribution downstream.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because this behavior is local extraction/structuring logic over transcript text, and the described failure can be triggered within one parser boundary. Rejection gate: Integration rejected since behavior text does not require real module handoff failure; System/Agentic/Workflow rejected because no OS/network/subprocess or model-output dependence is required by the behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments into the parsing function/object and assert exact assignee/task fields in the produced summary-input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this checks generated summary behavior at service boundaries, not one local function. System beats Integration because a fake summarizer would hide real API behavior. Counterbalance: Agentic beats System because correctness depends on LLM output quality (coverage of assigned attendees), and stubs would always pass if prefilled. Rejection gate: Workflow considered but rejected because behavior text targets one summary-output quality criterion, not a required chain of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real meeting transcript inputs with known attendee task assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because the behavior is routing/field-mapping logic once decisions are already identified; the failure is in local transformation, not cross-module infrastructure. Rejection gate: higher levels rejected since behavior text explicitly scopes to routing logic, not real network/OS, external APIs, or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Provide structured input containing pre-identified decisions to the routing component and assert outputs populate only the `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this validates live summary behavior, not isolated mapping code. System beats Integration because real model invocation is needed; mocks can force passing placement. Counterbalance: Agentic beats System because correctness depends on LLM extraction/placement quality for decisions from transcript content. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text focuses on one model-output property in the summary, not a required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts with clear decision statements to the real summarization model and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent as only narrative mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because chunking/merge orchestration crosses components. System beats Integration because real long-audio handling and external calls are needed to expose sequencing/merge failures. Counterbalance: Agentic floor applies since each segment relies on model outputs; Workflow beats Agentic because the behavior explicitly requires multiple model-involved steps in sequence (split -> per-segment processing -> merge coherence). Rejection gate: no concrete text disqualifies Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production split-sequential processing with real STT+LLM calls, then assert merged output is a single structured summary with cross-segment consistency and no dropped segment content.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is end-to-end pipeline behavior across modules. System beats Integration because it requires real upload/I/O and external API interactions. Counterbalance: Agentic floor applies due real transcription and summarization model calls; Workflow beats Agentic because the behavior explicitly spans multiple agentic stages in sequence across the whole pipeline. Rejection gate: no behavior text narrows scope to a single model step, so higher level is required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a true end-to-end run with a real meeting audio file in production-like conditions and assert autonomous completion plus valid structured output delivery without operator intervention.
